title: WILDS RxRx1 Batch-Shift Cellular Microscopy Dataset

data_urls.access_notes: Use WILDS package and dataset documentation for official split metadata.
Image datasets can have high disk I/O; plan local storage before full download.

instructions: Use the WILDS package to access RxRx1 and official batch-domain metadata.
Start with precomputed embeddings or a batch-stratified image sample for SPECTRA exploration.
Record experiment, plate, well, site, cell type, perturbation label, and split metadata.

scale.download_size: multi-GB microscopy image dataset; use sampled images or embeddings for repeated audits

recommended_axes.name: batch_and_plate_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Experiment, batch, plate, well, or acquisition-support similarity.

recommended_axes.name: morphology_embedding_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Frozen image-encoder or morphology-profile similarity to training examples.

recommended_axes.name: channel_intensity_style_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Train-fitted channel-intensity, staining, and imaging-style features.

useful_for: Batch-effect and acquisition-shift audits in microscopy.
Testing whether official batch shifts are one operating point on a morphology or style curve.
Comparing biological perturbation support against image-acquisition support.
leakage_risks: Fields from the same well or plate can leak acquisition context.
Batch, cell type, and perturbation labels can be confounded.
Image embeddings may encode acquisition artifacts more strongly than biology.
